knowledgeArticles.title: Entrapment Response Procedure - Gen2 Premier
knowledgeArticles.summary: Step-by-step procedure for responding to passenger entrapment in Gen2 Premier elevators, including communication protocols, dispatch procedures, and safe extraction methods.

sections.title: Initial Response (0-2 minutes)
sections.content:
1. Confirm entrapment location and elevator unit number
2. Establish communication with trapped passenger via intercom
3. Verify passenger condition - ask about injuries, mobility, and medical conditions
4. Reassure passenger that help is on the way
5. Log call start time for SLA tracking

sections.title: Dispatch Protocol
sections.content:
**Priority Dispatch Criteria:**
- Healthcare facilities: 1-hour SLA
- Commercial Tier 1: 2-hour SLA
- Residential/Standard: 4-hour SLA

**Dispatch Steps:**
1. Check technician availability in dispatch system
2. Identify nearest qualified technician with Gen2 Premier certification
3. Contact technician via mobile and confirm ETA
4. Update case with dispatch details and ETA

sections.title: Communication Cadence
sections.content:
- Call customer back every 5 minutes until technician arrives
- Send email confirmation with technician ETA within 5 minutes of dispatch
- If ETA exceeds SLA, escalate to Field Supervisor immediately
- Document all communications in case timeline

sections.title: Safe Extraction Methods
sections.content:
**Technician procedures (for reference only):**
1. Attempt remote reset via Otis ONE if equipped
2. Manual leveling procedure using machine room controls
3. Emergency manual release sequence
4. Fire service operation as last resort

**IMPORTANT:** Agents should never instruct customers on extraction procedures. All extraction must be performed by certified technicians.

knowledgeArticles.author: Technical Documentation Team
knowledgeArticles.demoNotes: KEY ENTRAPMENT KB - Copilot will surface this when handling HERO-001 entrapment case. Good for showing how KB articles guide agent actions.

knowledgeArticles.title: Healthcare Facility Emergency Protocols
knowledgeArticles.summary: Special handling procedures for elevator emergencies at healthcare facilities, including enhanced SLA requirements, patient safety considerations, and coordination with hospital security.

sections.title: Healthcare Priority Classification
sections.content:
Healthcare facilities receive highest priority response:

**Tier H1 - Critical Healthcare:**
- Hospitals with A&E departments
- Response time: 1 hour maximum
- 24/7 on-call technician required

**Tier H2 - Standard Healthcare:**
- Clinics and outpatient facilities
- Response time: 2 hours maximum

sections.title: Patient Impact Assessment
sections.content:
When taking healthcare facility calls, always assess:

1. **Is patient transport affected?** - Bed elevators require immediate priority
2. **Are there patients in the elevator?** - Escalate to supervisor immediately
3. **Is surgical/emergency access blocked?** - Coordinate with hospital incident command
4. **Are medical supplies affected?** - Pharmacy transport elevators are high priority

sections.title: Hospital Coordination
sections.content:
**Key Contacts:**
- Hospital Security (first point of contact)
- Facilities Management
- Incident Command (for major events)

**Communication:**
- Use medical terminology appropriately
- Maintain HIPAA awareness - don't discuss patients
- Coordinate arrival times with security for building access

sections.title: Documentation Requirements
sections.content:
Healthcare incidents require enhanced documentation:
- Exact time of incident
- Patient impact assessment
- Response time vs. SLA
- Resolution details
- Any safety incidents

All healthcare cases auto-flagged for quality review.

knowledgeArticles.author: Healthcare Vertical Team
knowledgeArticles.demoNotes: HEALTHCARE-SPECIFIC KB - Surfaces for Riverside Medical Centre cases. Shows industry-specific handling knowledge.

knowledgeArticles.title: SkyRise Door Operator Troubleshooting Guide
knowledgeArticles.summary: Comprehensive troubleshooting guide for SkyRise elevator door operator issues, including common symptoms, diagnostic steps, and resolution procedures.

sections.title: Common Door Operator Symptoms
sections.content:
**Symptom 1: Repeated Door Reversals**
- Door starts to close, then reverses multiple times
- Often indicates sensor obstruction or misalignment
- Check for debris in door track

**Symptom 2: Slow Door Operation**
- Doors move slower than normal
- May indicate motor wear or belt tension issues

**Symptom 3: Door Won't Close Completely**
- Doors stop short of full closure
- Check for track obstruction or limit sensor issues

sections.title: Agent Diagnostic Questions
sections.content:
Ask the customer:
1. Does the issue happen every time or intermittently?
2. Is there a specific floor where it's worse?
3. What time of day does it occur most? (temperature-related expansion)
4. Has anything been replaced or repaired recently?
5. Are passengers reporting any sounds when the issue occurs?

Document answers in case notes for technician.

sections.title: Temporary Mitigation
sections.content:
**If elevator is safe to operate:**
- Advise customer to notify users of slower door operation
- Recommend avoiding heavy traffic floors if possible

**If elevator unsafe:**
- Guide customer to lock off elevator at landing
- Place out-of-service signage
- Escalate for emergency dispatch

sections.title: Recurring Issue Protocol
sections.content:
If this is 3rd+ occurrence:
1. Pull historical maintenance records
2. Identify pattern in previous technician notes
3. Escalate to Field Supervisor for comprehensive inspection
4. Consider sensor replacement or track realignment
5. Flag for engineering review if pattern persists

knowledgeArticles.author: SkyRise Product Team
knowledgeArticles.demoNotes: DOOR TROUBLESHOOTING KB - Surfaces for HERO-002 Canary Wharf recurring door issue. Shows diagnostic guidance.

knowledgeArticles.title: Full Maintenance Contract Coverage Guide
knowledgeArticles.summary: Comprehensive guide to what is included and excluded in Full Maintenance service contracts, helping agents accurately answer coverage questions and identify billing errors.

sections.title: Components INCLUDED in Full Maintenance
sections.content:
**Always Covered:**
- Door operators and door panels
- Controllers and drive systems
- Safety devices and interlocks
- Cables, sheaves, and guides
- Machine and motor components
- Car operating panels
- Hall stations and indicators
- Routine PM labor and parts

**Category Summary:** If it's essential for elevator operation, it's covered.

sections.title: Components EXCLUDED
sections.content:
**Customer Responsibility:**
- Cab finishes and interiors (cosmetic)
- Light fixtures and bulbs
- Telephone equipment
- Signage and branding
- Vandalism repairs
- Water damage repairs
- Fire damage repairs

**Category Summary:** Cosmetic, communication, and damage from external causes.

sections.title: Common Coverage Questions
sections.content:
**Q: Is a door motor replacement covered?**
A: YES - Door operator components are always covered.

**Q: Is cab carpet replacement covered?**
A: NO - Cab finishes are customer responsibility.

**Q: Is a callback for entrapment covered?**
A: YES - All emergency responses are covered.

**Q: Is vandalism repair covered?**
A: NO - External damage is excluded.

sections.title: Billing Error Process
sections.content:
If a customer believes they were incorrectly billed:

1. Pull the work order and review service codes
2. Check if the work was coded as T&M vs. Contract
3. Compare parts replaced against coverage list
4. If error confirmed:
   - Submit correction request via billing portal
   - Reference work order and invoice numbers
   - Notify customer of credit timeline
5. If correctly billed:
   - Explain specific exclusion to customer
   - Offer to review contract upgrade options

knowledgeArticles.author: Contract Administration
knowledgeArticles.demoNotes: CONTRACT COVERAGE KB - Surfaces for HERO-004 Marriott billing dispute. Key for demonstrating accurate billing resolution.

knowledgeArticles.title: Otis ONE IoT Monitoring Package
knowledgeArticles.summary: Overview of Otis ONE connected elevator monitoring system, including features, benefits, and customer talking points for sales conversations.

sections.title: Otis ONE Overview
sections.content:
Otis ONE is our cloud-based IoT platform that transforms elevator maintenance:

**Key Capabilities:**
- Real-time performance monitoring
- Predictive maintenance alerts
- Remote diagnostics
- Usage analytics and reporting
- Building manager dashboard

**Supported Equipment:**
- Gen2 systems (all variants)
- SkyRise high-speed elevators
- NCE escalators
- Retrofit available for select legacy systems

sections.title: Customer Benefits
sections.content:
**Uptime Improvement:**
- Average 25% reduction in callbacks
- Predictive alerts prevent 60% of potential shutdowns

**Cost Savings:**
- Optimized maintenance scheduling
- Reduced emergency dispatch
- Energy usage monitoring

**Building Insights:**
- Traffic pattern analysis
- Peak usage reporting
- Tenant satisfaction metrics

sections.title: Installation Requirements
sections.content:
**New Installation:**
- Included with all Gen2 and SkyRise installations
- Activated during commissioning

**Retrofit:**
- Control system compatibility check required
- Typical installation: 2-4 hours per unit
- Network connectivity required (cellular or building ethernet)
- One-time installation fee + monthly subscription

sections.title: Talking Points for Sales Handoff
sections.content:
When customers express interest in Otis ONE:

1. Confirm their equipment compatibility
2. Ask about their current pain points (callbacks, energy costs, visibility)
3. Describe relevant case studies in their vertical
4. Offer to schedule a technical consultation
5. Create sales opportunity in CRM and assign to territory rep

**ROI Talking Point:** Average payback period is 18 months through reduced emergency calls and optimized energy usage.

knowledgeArticles.author: Otis ONE Product Marketing
knowledgeArticles.demoNotes: IOT/MODERNIZATION KB - Surfaces for HERO-003 The Shard modernization inquiry. Shows service-to-sales knowledge handoff.
